Title: UN: Scam losses in Asian regions up to US$114bil in 2025
Article:
BANGKOK: Victims of transnational online scams in East Asia, Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand lost up to US$114.1 billion last year, a UN report said on Tuesday, with estimated losses at least tripling from 2023.
Southeast Asia, especially Cambodia and Myanmar, has emerged as a regional hub for crime syndicates running fake romance and cryptocurrency investment schemes in which scammers – some willing, others trafficked – often working in fortified compounds defraud internet users around the world.
Online scams, mostly from investment fraud, across the specified regions cost victims an estimated US$88.3 billion to US$114.1 billion last year, according to the new report from the UN Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC).
The combined losses far exceed the US$18 billion-US$37 billion estimated to have been lost in 2023, and reflect "the dramatic scaling of this criminal economy", it said.
China, South Korea and Taiwan reported the largest losses from scams in the region in the past two years, each losing billions of dollars, the report said.
GLOBAL SPREAD
The cyberscam industry has ballooned in Southeast Asia in recent years, with organised criminal groups initially mostly targeting Chinese speakers before widening their reach and targeting victims globally.
They also recruit and traffick scammers from around the world, with individuals from at least 80 countries and territories found in scam compounds across the Mekong region, the report said.
AI-powered translation tools have made it easier to reach new audiences but recruits still post advertisements for staff speaking German, Polish, Dutch, Spanish, Italian, French, Swedish and Norwegian, as well as English, it said.
And when the scam centres are raided by authorities, some of the foreigners who work there take their newfound skills back home.
Under pressure from several countries, including the United States, Britain and China, authorities in the region say they are cracking down on the illicit industry.
Several alleged scam network bosses were extradited from Cambodia to China in the past year, after Washington and London sanctioned firms and individuals accused of operating far-reaching cyberscams fuelled by human trafficking.
The report authors said many operations continue despite the arrest of the kingpins, and that some of those released from raided centres take their skills elsewhere.
[[nid:1494187]]
"It's always a bit of a thin distinction between victims and whoever has been using their presence in the group to acquire criminal expertise that they're going to be able to sell," UNODC regional representative Delphine Schantz said.
"They're going back to their country of origin and they're tapping into some existing criminal networks," she told a news conference, noting that this was happening in Africa as well as Europe, particularly in the Balkans.
CRIMINAL ECOSYSTEM
The scam centres are only one part of a wider criminal ecosystem in Southeast Asia that has been transformed over the past decade, the UNODC said.
It encompasses the illegal drug trade – mostly methamphetamine – as well as child sexual exploitation and real estate investment, working through existing trade routes and hiding behind cryptocurrencies, the report said.
Organised criminals are "moving away from a fragmented system of locally rooted criminal syndicates toward an increasingly integrated, transnational, and technologically sophisticated criminal economy", it said.
The shift has challenged conventional law enforcement and threatened regional governance, as well as economic and social stability.
"Their operating model looks like corporate franchising: imagine specialised departments for laundering money, trafficking people, smuggling migrants, and harvesting data, all plugged into the same, service-based interconnected network," Schantz said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Central Asia's unbridled cosmetic surgery boom
Article:
AFTER two pregnancies, Diana Tergenbayeva dreamed of a breast lift. Instead, she was left with severe swelling, an open wound and a legal dispute with the surgeon who performed her botched procedure in Kyrgyzstan.
With a swollen face and a gash under her breast where "the stitches came undone" three times, Tergenbayeva barely resembles herself in the pictures taken after the surgery that she showed to AFP.
"I couldn't open my eyes, my oxygen saturation and blood pressure had dropped," she said, after developing an angioedema – a swelling in the under-skin tissue – from the medication she received.
Her case is just one of an increasing number of failed – sometimes fatal – procedures in Central Asia, where cosmetic surgery is experiencing a social media-propelled boom.
Tergenbayeva's surgeon was detained in September for smuggling medications into the country. Around 40 patients had lodged complaints.
But he has been released after his case was dismissed, and, to Tergenbayeva's horror, continues to practise.
"He presents himself as the best surgeon in Kyrgyzstan, but he's just a kind of 'butcher' of Kyrgyzstan," she said.
He "should have been imprisoned", she added.
The surgeon did not respond to an AFP request for comment.
Like many, Tergenbayeva, said she was "attracted by the beautiful photos on Instagram" of other cosmetic operations.
Official statistics are lacking, but media in Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan have reported an increasing number of victims.
The trend has empowered more women to speak up, especially after a Kazakh blogger with around a million Instagram followers died after undergoing liposuction in May.
Experts have attributed the cases to a lack of state regulation, the use of expired medicines and unqualified practitioners working illegally.
Authorities have acknowledged problems.
The Kyrgyz health ministry told AFP that the situation was "particularly worrying in Bishkek and Osh, where more than 1,300 companies are illegally carrying out their medical or pharmaceutical activities."
They have started inspections, leading to fines and closures of some premises that offer invasive procedures without proper permits or with outdated products.
But the effectiveness of the interventions is patchy.
[[nid:1494182]]
"Many beauticians often have a little too much freedom. They think they have learned and, without being surgeons, sometimes perform surgical procedures," Kyrgyz plastic surgeon Iskender Kachkinbayev told AFP.
"All medical interventions must take place in a medical setting, under no circumstances in an office," he said.
Despite the dangers, influencers with hundreds of thousands of followers on Instagram or TikTok promote private clinics or beauty salons that experts say are dubious.
A quick social media video can result in "10 patients for consultations tomorrow," Kachkinbayev said, warning that the offers "can sometimes be a real scam."
Jasmine Karipayeva, 27 from Kazakhstan, decided to have blepharoplasty – surgery to remove skin from the eyelids – after "coming across a video on TikTok", lured by low prices and pictures of professional-looking surgery.
"There is a quest for European standards. We have the impression that if we get our eyes done with open eyelids, they will look beautiful and natural," she told AFP when asked why she had undergone the procedure.
She immediately regretted the move.
"One eyelid is drooping, the other remains open. The doctor realised he had cut too deeply into one eye."
"No one can give me back my original appearance, because a lot of fat and skin have been removed," she said.
She has decided not to go through the ordeal of a court battle against the doctor.
Kyrgyz nurse Oksana Tikhaya is another victim of a botched procedure.
An operation to life the 48-year-old's eyelid after a minor stroke left her with huge swelling that covered her eye.
"You couldn't even get your little finger in there. It's been a year and a half, but my eye still feels strained and I constantly want to force it open," she told AFP.
She said she "regrets not having consulted the reviews and having rushed to this surgeon."
Tergenbayeva, who had the failed breast surgery, is also urging others to be cautious.
"Women should think twice before succumbing to the charm of all these Instagram beauties and going under the knife."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Smugglers hide RM577,280 worth of cigarettes in remote village hut
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: Smugglers are turning remote village huts into hidden storage facilities for contraband cigarettes before distributing them to local buyers.
Kelantan Customs director Mohd Hisham Mohd Nor said the tactic was uncovered after the department's Rantau Panjang enforcement team raided a hut in Kampung Kubang Bulat, Gunong, Bachok, on June 29.
During the 10.45pm raid, officers seized 640,000 sticks of smuggled cigarettes packed in 64 boxes, worth RM577,280, including RM449,280 in unpaid duties.
No arrests were made.
"We believe smugglers are changing their tactics by using wooden huts in remote villages.
"Investigations are ongoing to identify the hut's owner and those involved in the syndicate," he told a press conference today.
Hisham said initial investigations found that the hut was used to temporarily store the cigarettes before they were distributed to local buyers.
He said its isolated location, far from the main road, made it an ideal hiding place for the contraband.
"We believe the cigarettes were smuggled into the state through sea and river routes before being stored in the hut," he said.
Hisham said Customs officers had monitored the location for more than a week before conducting the raid.
He said it was the first case in Kelantan involving a wooden hut being used as a storage facility for smuggled cigarettes.
The case is being investigated under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India youth group says will not march again because police will 'hurt youth'
Article:
NEW DELHI: India's youth-led "cockroach" movement said on Tuesday it would continue its protest but will not march again, a day after scores of supporters were injured in clashes with police who tried to stop their march on parliament.
Delhi Police said nearly 180 people, including 118 security and police personnel, and 60 protesters, were injured on Monday when they clashed in central Delhi.
Thousands of protesters from Delhi and nearby cities and towns gathered to march on parliament on Monday, joining a movement that began as an online satire but has turned out to become the biggest challenge for Prime Minister Narendra Modi in his third term.
The months-old movement by the self-named Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has drawn the support of millions of young, Gen Z Indians who are demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over examination paper leaks that affected more than 2 million students in May.
Seventy protesters had been detained and legal action would be initiated against them, police said in a statement late on Monday.
Early on Tuesday, about 150-200 protesters gathered at the protest site of Jantar Mantar in central Delhi, chanting slogans amid a mild drizzle. Security remained tight with paramilitary personnel patrolling parts of central Delhi and barricades slowing traffic.
"We had to call off the protest yesterday because we did not want more youth to be injured," CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke told a press conference.
"We will not march again because the police will hurt the youth again," he said, adding that more than 150 protesters were undergoing treatment in hospital.
Reuters could not independently verify that number.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tabung Haji in much stronger financial position following recovery efforts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) is in a much stronger financial position now compared with 2018 following continuous recovery and restructuring efforts.
In a statement today, the pilgrims' fund board said the stronger position is reflected in its ability to maintain the cost of performing the haj for three consecutive years (2024–2026) as well as its announcement of the highest profit distribution in eight years for 2025.
TH recorded a significant increase in profit distribution rate to 3.5 per cent for the 2025 financial year (FY2025) compared with 1.25 per cent for FY2018, benefiting more than 9 million depositors.
According to TH, the recovery and restructuring measures undertaken in 2018 were strategic decisions aimed at safeguarding depositors' savings and ensuring the fund's long-term financial sustainability.
At that time, the gap between assets and liabilities, first identified in 2017, had continued to rise to about RM10 billion, which could possibly result in TH failing to meet the requirements set out under the Tabung Haji Act 1995.
This year, TH has reacquired two assets offered by Urusharta Jamaah Sdn Bhd based on commercial-based considerations in line with its Strategic Asset Allocation, including a parcel of land at the Tun Razak Exchange at its current market value of RM270 million compared with its premium value of RM400 million in 2018.
TH said it has also reacquired oil palm  plantation firm UJ Estates Holdings Sdn Bhd for RM695 million following the company's income recovery and stable cash flow.
TH added that its continuous recovery plan over the past eight years has also made it blance sheet stronger and more resilient, enabling it to continue fulfilling its mandate of managing the savings of the Muslim community while consistently delivering world-class haj services.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Sabah-based NGO president arrested over alleged RM2 million misappropriation
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The president of a Sabah-based non-governmental organisation (NGO) has been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for allegedly misappropriating RM2 million allocated for the construction of a cultural hall.
The male suspect, in his 60s, was arrested at about 10am today after turning up to give his statement at the Sabah MACC office.
A source said preliminary investigations revealed that the suspect allegedly committed the offence in 2022 by misappropriating funds obtained from the Finance Ministry for the construction of a cultural hall and gallery in the state.
"The suspect is believed to have applied for the allocation and carried out the construction of the hall on his own without obtaining the approval of the NGO's supreme council members," the source said.
Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah confirmed the arrest when contacted, saying the case is being investigated under Section 23 of the MACC Act 2009 for abuse of power.
He added that the suspect is expected to be charged at the Kota Kinabalu Special Corruption Court on Thursday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GOF detains driver after car chase, seizes air rifle and ball bearings
Article:
TUMPAT: A 32-year-old man was arrested after he allegedly rammed a General Operations Force (GOF) vehicle while attempting to evade arrest in Kampung Parit Bendang Morak here yesterday.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspect was detained by a GOF team at 5.30am.
He said the patrol team had spotted a car being driven in a suspicious manner before approaching the vehicle for inspection.
"Upon noticing the presence of our personnel, the driver attempted to flee and rammed the front of the GOF vehicle.
"The suspect then lost control of the car, which veered into a nearby padi field, before he was detained," he said.
Radzi said checks on the vehicle uncovered a homemade air rifle on the front passenger seat and a bottle containing 61 steel ball bearings believed to be used as ammunition.
The total seizure is valued at RM12,061, comprising the car estimated at RM10,000, the homemade air rifle worth RM2,000, and the ball bearings worth RM61.
"Initial investigations revealed that the suspect claimed the homemade air rifle was made by a man known as 'Eric' and had been in his possession for the past three years.
"He allegedly used the weapon to hunt civets, which were later sold around Tumpat for RM60 per kg," he added.
Radzi said the suspect also claimed the car belonged to his father.
The case is being investigated under Section 8 of the Firearms Act 1960.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Smart manufacturing fund to unlock SME growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry and the Securities Commission (SC) have launched a new Smart Manufacturing scheme to provide greater access to capital and support small and medium enterprises (SMEs) and mid-tier companies (MTCs) in strategic sectors.
The scheme, under the New Industrial Master Plan 2030's (NIMP 2030) Strategic Co-Investment Fund (NIMP CoSIF), is aimed at supporting business expansion and accelerating smart manufacturing adoption in strategic industries through enhanced government co-investment.
Under the new scheme, the government will contribute RM2 for every RM1 raised from private investors, giving the Smart Manufacturing category a 2:1 co-investment ratio until 2030.
The scheme targets eligible companies undertaking smart manufacturing transformation, including projects involving automation, Industry 4.0 technologies, digitalisation and other productivity-enhancing initiatives.
The ministry and the SC have also temporarily increased the co-investment ratio for businesses in the "Other Sectors" category from 1:2 to 1:1 until the end of 2027.
Under the revised ratio, the government will match private investment ringgit-for-ringgit, providing SMEs and MTCs with greater financing support amid continued economic and market uncertainties.
The move also aligns the funding ratio for "Other Sectors" with that of the priority sectors and new growth sectors under NIMP 2030.
The two agencies said the enhanced funding support is intended to accelerate capital deployment, strengthen business resilience and help companies scale within the industrial sectors identified under NIMP 2030.
"With the enhanced ratio, the government will contribute RM1 for every RM1 raised, aligning the co-investment ratio with that of the 'Priority Sectors' and 'New Growth Sectors' under NIMP 2030.
"The ministry and SC believe that this strategic intervention will help companies sustain growth, strengthen resilience and continue scaling within NIMP sectors," they said.
NIMP CoSIF is a public-private co-investment programme that channels government funding alongside private capital into businesses operating across the 25 sectors identified under NIMP 2030.
The fund leverages equity crowdfunding and peer-to-peer financing platforms to provide alternative financing for Malaysian businesses.
Since its launch, the programme has facilitated public-private fundraising to support the growth of SMEs and MTCs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] NGO secretary, treasurer arrested over alleged RM5 million money laundering
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  A non-governmental organisation (NGO) secretary and treasurer have been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for allegedly laundering RM5 million in funds.
The funds are believed to have been misappropriated from the organisation they served.
The two suspects, a man and a woman in their 30s, were detained when they turned up at the MACC headquarters to have their statements recorded about 10am today.
An MACC source said preliminary investigations found that the pair had allegedly conspired to receive, possess, transfer, convert and use proceeds of unlawful activities linked to the misappropriation of the NGO's funds for their personal benefit between 2021 and 2023.
The source said the alleged money laundering involved funds amounting to about RM5 million.
MACC Anti-Money Laundering Division director Datuk Norhaizam Muhammad confirmed the arrests when contacted.
He said the case was being investigated under Section 4(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLATFPUAA 2001).
Norhaizam added that both suspects were expected to be charged at the Shah Alam Sessions Court on July 29.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: French lawmakers vote on social media ban for children
Article:
PARIS: French lawmakers vote on Tuesday on a law that aims to ban social media access for children under the age of 15, joining countries around the world in considering a move unpopular with many teenagers but supported by some parents and teachers.
If it is adopted, France would follow Australia, where the world's first ban for under-16s on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into force in December, as concerns grow over the impact social media has on the health and safety of minors.
President Emmanuel Macron, who in April urged young people to switch off their phones and read in order to become better citizens, wants the law in place in time for the start of the next academic year.
France's draft legislation, which senators and deputies agreed on at committee level late on Monday, requires social media platforms to set up systems of age verification approved by the French privacy regulator.
The vote in parliament is due in the mid- to late afternoon.
With the proposed legislation, children younger than 15 would not be allowed to open a social media account from Sept 1. Social media platforms would have another four months to close accounts already open.
Online encyclopaedias and educational websites would remain available to children and teenagers.
Social media platforms generally oppose blanket bans and stress they already have measures to protect younger users, including age restrictions. But they have also pledged to comply where governments enact bans.
Countries across Europe and beyond are considering ways to restrict social media after becoming increasingly aware of the risks to children.
The European Commission is working on its own regulations to rein in social media's business models to protect young people, and it will have a say on whether France's bill respects existing European legislation.
"We left you in this jungle and it robbed you of your attention," Macron told teenagers at a high school in April, speaking of the lack of rules on social media. "We need to slow down and help you become adults, and above all citizens."
"That's why what we want to do is say that before 15 years old, no more social media," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends lower on profit-taking despite positive market breadth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index closed marginally lower on Tuesday, weighed down by continued profit-taking, although the positive market breadth indicated that selective buying interest remained intact.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 1.92 points, or 0.11 per cent, to close at 1,720.37 from Monday's close of 1,722.29.
The index opened 3.33 points higher at 1,725.62, and moved between 1,712.66 and 1,726.88 throughout the day.
On the broader market, advancers beat decliners 579 to 512, while 539 counters were unchanged, 1,105 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover increased to 3.48 billion units valued at RM2.79 billion from 3.27 billion units valued at RM2.21 billion on Monday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said regional equities advanced as improving prospects for diplomatic negotiations in West Asia eased crude oil prices and boosted overall risk appetite.
"Looking ahead, investors will closely monitor earnings from major US technology companies, including Alphabet, Tesla and Intel, for confirmation that heavy artificial intelligence (AI)-related investments are translating into stronger revenue growth and profitability," he told Bernama.
On the domestic front, Thong expects the benchmark index to remain in a consolidation phase within the 1,710–1,740 range this week.
He said investors are likely to stay selective ahead of key US corporate earnings, while improving global risk sentiment and softer crude oil prices could provide near-term support.
"Nevertheless, sustained foreign fund inflows, resilient domestic economic fundamentals and continued accumulation of fundamentally strong large-cap stocks should help cushion downside risks.
"We expect sector rotation to remain active, with investors favouring companies offering resilient earnings growth and attractive valuations," he said.
Among the heavyweight counters, Maybank and IHH Healtcare were both flat at RM11.02 and RM8.50, respectively, Public Bank inched up one sen to RM5.20, Tenaga Nasional gained 26 sen to RM14.56, while CIMB slid seven sen to RM7.68.
Among active stocks, Main Market debutant Stratus Global surged RM1.31 to RM2.11, VS Industry added 2.5 sen to 25 sen, Zetrix AI edged up one sen to 73 sen, Dagang NeXchange gained two sen to 46.5 sen, and Aimax was unchanged at one sen.
Among top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries garnered RM2.18 to RM46.84, Mi Technovation soared RM1.05 sen to RM5.92, Nestle advanced 46 sen to RM93.12, UMS Integration rose by 41 sen to RM8.19, and Unisem was 37 sen higher at RM4.85.
United Plantations led the decliners, slipping 86 sen to RM33.74, Kuala Lumpur Kepong dipped 44 sen to RM21.14, Fraser & Neave lost 34 sen to RM28.80, Petronas Gas fell 22 sen to RM17.40, and Petronas Dagangan erased 16 sen to RM19.50.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Britain's new PM to cut taxes on electricity bills
Article:
BENGALURU: The government of new British Prime Minister Andy Burnham will cut taxes on electricity bills, Downing Street said in a statement on Tuesday, as he attempts to deliver on his promises to ease a cost-of-living crisis.
The British government is planning to remove the value-added tax (VAT) from domestic electricity bills from Oct 1, the statement said.
The cost of this action for this financial year will be funded from the cancellation of the government's £1.8 billion (US$2.42 billion) digital ID programme, Downing Street said.
Burnham's office had said earlier that one of his first moves as Britain's next leader will be to scrap government plans for a digital ID scheme.
The digital ID programme is a plan for all employees to hold a digital identity document, a scheme designed to tackle illegal migration but deemed a "fiasco" by a cross-party committee of lawmakers.
As Britain's new prime minister, Burnham wants to move quickly to show British politics can work and improve living standards after years of stagnation.
"Westminster has not been working for people for too long, with families struggling with the cost of living," Burnham said.
"That needs to change," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MGB wins RM44.7mil Saudi contract, second direct award from ROSHN
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MGB Bhd has won a RM44.67 million contract in Saudi Arabia for mechanical, electrical and plumbing (MEP) and finishing works at the Marafy Al Arous residential development in Jeddah, strengthening its presence in the kingdom's fast-growing construction sector.
The contract was awarded to its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, MGB International For Industry (MGBI), by the ROSHN Group Company and covers MEP and finishing works for 75 villa units.
The project is scheduled for completion within 10 months and includes mobilisation, engineering, construction, testing, commissioning and final handover.
The latest award follows an earlier RM35.91 million contract secured by MGBI from ROSHN for the construction and engineering works on the same 75 villas.
Together, the two contracts bring MGBI's total contract value for the Marafy Al Arous project to about RM80.59 million, giving MGB's international division a confidence boost in its ability to continuously secure projects within the Kingdom consistently.
MGB, a construction, property development and industrialised building system (IBS) precast concrete manufacturer, said the repeat award reflects ROSHN's continued confidence in the group's project delivery capabilities while reinforcing its expansion strategy in Saudi Arabia.
Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San, the group executive chairman of MGB, said this latest award from ROSHN marks another positive milestone in MGB's progress within Saudi Arabia.
"Our journey in the Kingdom began with leveraging our expertise in the manufacturing, supply and installation of IBS precast concrete products.
"Today, we have successfully progressed to undertaking projects as a main contractor, reflecting the confidence placed in our technical capabilities, execution excellence and commitment to quality," he said in a statement.
Lim thanked ROSHN for their continued confidence in MGBI.
He said Saudi Arabia remains a key pillar of MGB's international growth strategy as the group seeks to strengthen its local partnerships and support the kingdom's Vision 2030 development agenda.
Since entering the kingdom in 2023 through the manufacture, supply and installation of IBS precast concrete products, MGB has progressively expanded its role from supplier to main contractor and is targeting larger construction projects as well as selected property development opportunities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Education must produce critical thinkers, not just AI-ready graduates, says Zambry
Article:
IPOH: Artificial intelligence (AI) may be able to provide answers in seconds, but it can never shape a person's character or moral compass.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said this was why Malaysia's higher education system must remain firmly rooted in humanity and ethics, even as universities accelerate efforts to prepare graduates for an increasingly AI-driven future.
"The purpose of education is not for you to memorise or simply find data. The purpose of education is to cultivate the mind.
"Education teaches people how to think critically about everything around them. AI may provide solutions and quick answers, but it cannot develop the character we need as human beings," he said.
He said this at the "Education Matters: Social Empowerment" session of the 8th World Conference on Islamic Thought and Civilisation here today. The event was opened by Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah.
Other panellists included Yayasan Petronas chief executive officer Shariah Nelly Francis, Professor of Islamic Political Thought at the National Islamic University, Jakarta, Professor Dr M. Din Syamsuddin, and Madina Institute US founding director Shaykh Dr Muhammad Yahya Al-Ninowy.
Zambry said universities should not merely produce graduates equipped with technical knowledge, but individuals with strong values, critical thinking skills and a sense of social responsibility.
He said this philosophy underpins the ministry's new 10-year Higher Education Blueprint (2026-2035), which seeks to balance intellectual excellence with moral and human development.
"We are trying to strike a balance between human capital development and moral development because intellectual capability alone is not sufficient.
"Ethical values must be infused into our education system if we want to prepare a generation capable of facing the uncertainties brought about by AI and other emerging technologies," he said.
Zambry said education had always been the cornerstone of Malaysia's national development, noting that successive governments had consistently made it the country's largest budgetary priority.
He said the nation's transformation from an agriculture-based economy into one driven by knowledge and technology was possible because of sustained investment in education.
"Since independence, education has remained at the centre of national policy. Malaysia has consistently allocated the largest share of the national budget to education because it is the foundation of nation-building," he said.
Zambry added that universities must move beyond rigid academic structures and embrace a more interdisciplinary approach.
He said students, particularly those pursuing science and engineering, should also be exposed to the humanities, arts and social sciences to develop well-rounded graduates.
"For years, we have focused on STEM. Perhaps it is time to rethink STEM by introducing another dimension — the arts.
"Technical expertise alone is no longer enough. Universities must also cultivate personality, character and the ability to think critically," he said.
Zambry said education should never be reduced to preparing students solely for the job market.
Instead, he said, universities must produce graduates capable of questioning assumptions, understanding society and making ethical decisions.
"AI can give you information, but it cannot teach wisdom, conscience or humanity.
"That remains the responsibility of education, and that is why humanity is placed at the centre of our new higher education blueprint," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: PM can sack BN ministers if unhappy with performance
Article:
SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim can dismiss ministers from the coalition if he is unhappy with their performance.
Zahid, who is also the deputy prime minister, said there was no reason for BN ministers to resign voluntarily.
"The prime minister warned that those who speak ill of other ministers or ministries during the election campaign should resign," Zahid said at the Amanat TPM event with the Negri Sembilan Community Development Department today.
"My answer is that there is no need to ask anyone to resign because the prime minister is the one who appointed the ministers. If he doesn't like us, he can just sack us.
"However, he wouldn't have the heart to sack us because all of us work very hard.
"The power is in his hands. This is an election campaign, and we haven't interfered with Putrajaya at all."
On Sunday, Anwar warned that ministers, deputy ministers, and heads of federal agencies
must resign
if they used their positions to attack fellow unity government partners during state election campaigns.
Anwar said while parties aligned at the federal level were free to contest against one another in state elections, those holding federal office must maintain discipline and avoid turning their positions into political platforms.
In response, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said he was
ready to step down
from his cabinet post if ordered to do so by the prime minister.
Mohamad, who is also BN deputy chairman, added that no BN member had criticised the federal government throughout the campaign period so far.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 12,000 Cambodians seek medical care in Malaysia: MHTC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nearly 12,000 Cambodian patients sought medical treatment in Malaysia in 2025, contributing RM30 million in healthcare travel revenue.
The Malaysia Healthcare Travel Council (MHTC) said Cambodia's growing trust in the country's healthcare reflects rising demand for quality overseas medical treatment.
This reinforces Malaysia's position as a leading healthcare travel destination in the region.
"While Cambodia remains an emerging market compared to Malaysia's more established healthcare travel sources, its steady growth demonstrates increasing awareness and trust among Cambodian patients who are actively seeking quality healthcare beyond their borders," it said.
MHTC said Cambodia's healthcare landscape has evolved significantly over the past decade, with rising healthcare awareness and growing demand for specialised treatments driving more patients to seek cross-border healthcare services.
It said the evolving healthcare landscape presents a significant opportunity for Malaysia.
This is supported by a network of 91 member hospitals comprising internationally accredited medical institutions, multidisciplinary Centres of Excellence and experienced specialists across a wide range of healthcare services.
It said Malaysia continues to attract international patients seeking treatments in areas including oncology, cardiology, orthopaedics, fertility and reproductive medicine, gastroenterology, health screening and preventive care.
MHTC said Malaysia's healthcare ecosystem is also supported by its "Healing Meets Hospitality" proposition, which emphasises patient-centred care, empathy and personalised support throughout the treatment journey.
MHTC recently organised the inaugural Malaysia Healthcare Expo (MHX) Phnom Penh, making Cambodia the first market outside Indonesia to host the consumer-focused healthcare exhibition.
The expo brought Malaysian healthcare providers and specialists closer to Cambodian communities, allowing patients to explore treatment options and receive professional advice before travelling to Malaysia for care.
"The decision to invest in Cambodia reflects a broader strategy to strengthen Malaysia's presence in emerging healthcare travel markets across the region.
"As awareness of Malaysia's healthcare capabilities continues to grow, Cambodia is expected to play an increasingly important role in supporting the industry's long-term growth," it said.
It said the impact extends beyond healthcare providers, as every international patient contributes to a broader economic ecosystem involving airlines, hotels, transportation, retail and tourism-related services.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Documents in fatal MEX crash handed over to army captain's lawyer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The prosecution today handed over documents related to two charges against an army captain linked to a drink-driving crash that killed a Bangladeshi man on Maju Expressway (MEX) in April.
Lawyer Muhammad Nazreen Jaafar Abdullah, who is representing Jad Faid Arhan, 31, confirmed receiving the chemist report, his client's alcohol level results, medical reports of the crash victims and photographs of the accident scene.
The documents were handed over by deputy public prosecutor Batrisyia Mohd Khusri during a mention proceeding before magistrate Aliaa Anwar.
After the proceedings, Nazreen said he was still awaiting several documents from the prosecution at another magistrate's court in relation to a murder charge involving the death of Bangladeshi Muzahid Millad, 22, a popular gaming content creator known as "Advance Gaming".
He said once the document handover process was completed, both cases would be consolidated and transferred to the High Court for trial.
Both magistrate's courts fixed Sept 11 for further mention.
Jad, dressed in a red collared T-shirt and jeans, was brought from Sungai Buloh Prison for the proceedings.
The army artillery division captain was denied bail after he was charged with murder and drink-driving offences on April 30.
Several of Jad's family members were present in court during both proceedings.
Nazreen said his client was doing well.
Jad is accused of causing the death of Muzahid about 5.30am on April 23 at Km1.9 of the MEX from the Salak Selatan direction towards Jalan Tun Razak.
No plea was recorded for the murder charge as such cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
The charge, framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years.
If the death penalty is not imposed, the offender will be liable to not less than 12 strokes of the rotan.
Jad has pleaded not guilty to two charges of driving a Ford Fiesta while under the influence of alcohol.
The offence allegedly caused him to lose control of the vehicle, resulting in injuries to e-hailing passenger Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, who is Muzahid's wife, and serious injuries to e-hailing driver Jailani Sapih, 41.
The charges were framed under Section 44(1A)(b) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for driving with alcohol levels exceeding the prescribed limit.
The offence carries a jail term of between seven and 10 years and a fine of not less than RM30,000 and not more than RM50,000 upon conviction.
It was previously reported that the accused had veered into the opposite lane before colliding with the e-hailing vehicle carrying the couple.
Muzahid died at the scene.
The couple had been travelling from Kuala Lumpur International Airport to the city.
Police said breathalyser tests conducted on the accused showed alcohol levels exceeding the legal limit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ahmad Maslan: BN to battle PH in states, partners at federal level
Article:
SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional will continue to compete against Pakatan Harapan at the state level while maintaining its partnership with the coalition at the federal level.
Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said this dynamic reflects a new political maturity, even if it appears somewhat unusual.
"What is happening in Negri Sembilan previously occurred in Johor. Although we are not aligned at the state level, we remain together in the federal government," he told reporters after visiting the Communications Ministry Media Centre here today.
"This will continue in Sarawak and Melaka. In Melaka, we are not with PH, and I do not see the Sarawak state government aligning with PH."
Ahmad, who is also the deputy works minister, said the approach was adopted based on the preferences of their respective state leaderships.
"There is an element of strangeness to this situation, but it demonstrates political maturity," he said.
"This happens overseas where state and federal governments do not align politically. In Malaysia, it began in Sabah when Warisan, which was aligned with the Federal government, was not aligned at the state level.
"We will continue this approach of competing in the states and cooperating at the federal level. This is a new political maturity."
On the understanding between BN and Perikatan Nasional at the federal level, Ahmad said it would depend on political developments in Negri Sembilan and Melaka.
"We do not know when the next general election will be held as the parliamentary term ends on Dec 19, 2027. We will wait and see if the understanding between BN and PN continues," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Rival parties share neighbourly spirit despite Juasseh three-cornered contest
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Despite a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election, the Juasseh constituency has become an unlikely symbol of political camaraderie, with the operations centres of three rival parties located just a short walk from one another.
A visit to Juasseh Tengah found that the operations centres of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Bersatu are separated by only about eight steps.
Directly opposite them is Barisan Nasional's (BN) operations centre, about 20 steps from both PH and Bersatu.
Trader Norizan Mohamed Yaacob, 51, said the close proximity of the three operations centres reflected the cordial relationship among party workers despite the keen electoral contest.
"I find it quite unique because although they are competing against each other, their operations centres are so close together, and they can still sit down and have meals together.
"There are no arguments or hostility between them.
"Usually, the party machineries even gather for dinner at the Bersatu operations centre at night," he said when met here today.
Juasseh is witnessing a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election, with BN's Datuk Ismail Lasim taking on Mohd Aidil Abdullah of PH and Mohd Zuhaimi Md Yusof of Bersatu.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KTMB adds six KTM Komuter Northern Sector services from Friday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) will introduce an improved KTM Komuter Northern Sector timetable from Friday, adding six daily services to cater to growing passenger demand and enhance travel comfort, particularly during peak hours.
In a statement today, KTMB said the additional services comprise two trains for the Padang Besar-Butterworth-Padang Besar route and four for the Ipoh-Butterworth-Ipoh route.
The increase will bring the total number of daily services from 56 to 62.
KTMB said waiting times on the Ipoh-Butterworth route during the morning peak would be reduced to 55 minutes from more than 120 minutes previously.
The rail operator has also extended the operating hours of the last train to provide passengers with greater travel flexibility.
The last train from Ipoh to Butterworth will now depart at 7.45pm, compared with 6.35pm previously, while the final Butterworth-Ipoh service will leave at 8.10pm instead of 7.10pm.
KTMB said waiting times on the Padang Besar-Butterworth route during the evening peak would be halved to 30 minutes from 60 minutes.
"The enhanced timetable is expected to benefit about 20,000 passengers daily, or 7.3 million passengers annually, particularly those travelling for work, education and other daily activities along the KTM Komuter Northern Sector corridor," read the statement.
KTMB said the initiative reflected its commitment to strengthening the national rail network while supporting the government's Road to Rail agenda, which encourages more people to switch from private vehicles to public transport for their daily commute.
The new timetable is available on the KITS STYLE application and KTMB's official website. Passengers can also obtain more information through KTMB's official social media platforms or by contacting its call centre at 03-9779 1200.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zico Trust partners Suriana to support disadvantaged children in Desa Mentari
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Zico Trust (M) Berhad has adopted Pusat Akademik dan Latihan 5 (PAL 5), a community learning centre in Desa Mentari, to provide holistic educational and psychosocial support for disadvantaged children.
The initiative, managed by Suriana Welfare Society Malaysia, will see Zico Trust funding a comprehensive suite of programmes including academic enrichment, digital learning, life skills development, and community engagement.
Zico Trust (M) Berhad chief financial officer Dinesh Menon said the firm was honoured to play a role in creating tangible opportunities for the B40 community.
"We believe that education and community development can help create stronger opportunities for everyone, and we are honoured to work with Suriana in making a practical difference where it matters most," he said in a statement.
The PAL centres were established by Suriana Welfare Society to provide children in low-cost housing areas with safe, structured environments.
These centres offer free tuition in core subjects such as English, Bahasa Malaysia, and Mathematics, while also focusing on parent empowerment.
Suriana Welfare Society Malaysia founder and chairman Dr James Nayagam said the support from the corporate sector is vital in breaking the cycle of poverty.
"For many children in Desa Mentari, the PAL centre is more than a place to study, it is a safe space where they are supported, encouraged, and given the chance to imagine a different future.
"Consistent and lasting support allows us to walk alongside these children in their academic growth and personal development," he said.
Beyond its financial commitment to the centre, Zico Trust will also leverage its corporate expertise by conducting periodic financial literacy workshops for the parents and families of Desa Mentari.
These sessions will cover essential topics such as budgeting, debt management, savings, and awareness of financial scams.
The partnership highlights a growing trend of collaborative efforts between the corporate sector and grassroots organisations to address socio-economic challenges in urban poor areas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aminuddin urges voters to study Pakatan Harapan's state election manifesto
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Pakatan Harapan hopes voters in Negri Sembilan will closely examine its state election manifesto, which prioritises all segments of society, particularly the M40 and B40 income groups.
Negri Sembilan PH chairman and acting Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the coalition's leadership has demonstrated a strong commitment to safeguarding the welfare of the people.
"We certainly hope voters will look at and study the PH manifesto," he said during house-to-house campaigning at Ladang Sekang Linggi here today.
"What matters most is the people and what we intend to provide for them. While previous initiatives were limited to the B40 group, we have now expanded them to the middle-income group so that everyone can share in overall well-being."
Under its manifesto themed 'Kekal Harapan', PH outlined 10 key commitments, including expanding educational opportunities to the M40 group.
Aminuddin said the current focus of PH leaders and candidates is to ensure the manifesto's commitments are clearly communicated to voters.
He added that PH's track record in governing the state since 2018 demonstrates its dedication to fulfilling every pledge made in its manifesto.
Commenting on his campaign, the Port Dickson member of parliament, who is contesting the Sikamat state seat, said operations were running smoothly and that he aimed to meet every voter in the constituency.
"The campaign momentum is good. Some residents have asked us to resolve minor local issues such as drainage, leaking roofs, and stagnant water in their compounds," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 10 workers die in hydroelectric project tunnel collapse in northeast India
Article:
HYDERABAD: At least 10 workers were killed after an under-construction tunnel at a hydroelectric project collapsed in India's northeastern state of Sikkim, with naturally occurring toxic gas hampering rescue operations, local media reported on Tuesday.
About 25 workers were inside the tunnel when it collapsed on Monday at the National Hydroelectric Power Corporation's Teesta Stage VI Hydroelectric Project in Samardung village, Namchi district.
Rescue efforts at the site were hampered by the presence of naturally occurring toxic gas underground as some rescuers experienced dizziness and loss of consciousness, reports said.
Sikkim Chief Minister Prem Singh Tamang said in social media posts that he had informed Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah about the situation following the disaster. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Sit down!' Australian politician berates dog on live TV
Article:
SYDNEY: A top lawmaker from Australia's far-right One Nation party has grabbed headlines after exploding at a misbehaving dog as he was grilled on live TV.
Former deputy prime minister Barnaby Joyce was being interviewed on national broadcaster ABC on Monday night when he interrupted the one-on-one to shriek "sit down!"
"Sorry, it's the dog," he told the journalist.
The errant pooch then interrupted the proceedings again, with Joyce saying: "I do apologise for this dog, it's just started to bail up."
"Don't worry about the dog, just answer the question," replied journalist Sarah Ferguson, who was grilling Joyce on his party leader Pauline Hanson's recent meeting with far-right British figurehead Tommy Robinson.
"Don't be rude. Otherwise, this interview will come to a grinding halt, Sarah," Joyce snapped back.
It's not the first time the cattle-farming politician has fallen foul of man's best friend – he once threatened to kill Johnny Depp's dogs.
His outburst drew ire across Australia, with Victoria state lawmaker Georgie Purcell of the Animal Justice Party writing on social media: "if you think this is a normal way to speak to your dog, sit down!"
Asked about the feedback, Joyce told the Sydney Morning Herald the outcry reflected a divide between the cities and rural Australians.
"People in the regions laugh about it," he said.
"People in the inner city think it's a war crime."
Opinion polls show One Nation, for decades a fringe group led by provocateur Hanson, has become Australia's most popular party in recent months.
Hanson has defended her decision to appear on a podcast with Robinson, a former football hooligan turned anti-Islam activist whose profile has soared in recent years.
She drew outrage back home for telling Robinson the end of the "White Australia" policy – which restricted non-white immigration – in the 1970s was partly responsible for the country's current ills.
Hanson later denied she was calling for a return to that policy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang leads seabass aquaculture output, stresses MyGAP compliance
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL: Penang produced 77 per cent of Malaysia's aquaculture seabass output in 2025, with authorities urging strict compliance with Good Aquaculture Practices (MyGAP).
Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said Penang's position as the country's leading seabass producer must be supported by good farming practices, including compliance with MyGAP, prudent antibiotic management and effective farm biosecurity.
"Compliance with MyGAP, prudent antibiotic management and biosecurity controls are essential to ensure aquaculture products are safe, of high quality and meet market requirements," he said during the MyGAP and Antibiotic Management Engagement Session for the aquaculture industry here today.
Malaysia's aquaculture industry is a vital and fast-growing contributor to the nation's food security and agricultural economy, accounting for approximately 30 per cent of total fisheries production.
As wild fish stocks face increasing pressure from overfishing, aquaculture has become an essential source of seafood, producing a diverse range of marine, brackish-water, and freshwater species, including vannamei shrimp, tilapia, and high-value marine finfish.
Adnan said Penang produced 44,596.46 metric tonnes of aquaculture seabass worth RM562.51 million in 2025, accounting for 77 per cent of the national output.
The Department of Fisheries (DOF) recorded Malaysia's total seabass production at 58,221.38 metric tonnes worth RM755.27 million last year.
Penang's seabass production rose from 39,690.39 metric tonnes worth RM489.55 million in 2024, when it accounted for about 65 per cent of the country's total aquaculture seabass output.
The latest figures represented a 12.36 per cent increase in production volume and a 14.9 per cent rise in production value compared with the previous year.
Apart from seabass, Penang recorded total aquaculture production of 54,767.3 metric tonnes worth RM1.14 billion in 2024.
DOF data shows there are 105 pond seabass farmers in Seberang Prai, comprising 93 operators in Seberang Prai Selatan, 11 in Seberang Prai Tengah and one in Seberang Prai Utara.
Another 17 white shrimp and tiger prawn farmers also operate in the area.
On exports, Adnan said the department is preparing for a technical field inspection by Thailand's Department of Fisheries on Malaysia's aquaculture export requirements.
The preparations involve collaboration between DOF, the Health Ministry, the Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority, the Border Control and Protection Agency and the Malaysian Quarantine and Inspection Services Department to ensure compliance with biosecurity, antibiotic management, product traceability and food safety standards.
He said DOF would continue monitoring and guiding industry players to ensure the country's aquaculture production meets food safety requirements, export market standards and consumer interests.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US-targeted scam syndicate busted in Kuantan: 14 foreigners among 16 detained
Article:
KUANTAN: Sixteen individuals, including 14 foreigners, believed to have operated a scam call centre targeting victims in the United States were arrested here yesterday.
The suspects, aged between 24 and 51, comprised eight women and eight men. They were detained around 11pm by a team from the Pahang Commercial Crime Investigation Department.
Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said intelligence gathering and surveillance led police to raid the premises, where they arrested two Taiwanese men, 12 Filipinos and two Malaysian men.
"The premises served as a call centre, where the suspects impersonated police officers to target unsuspecting victims in the United States. The syndicate would convince victims to part with their money using various modus operandi.
"Investigations revealed that the Taiwanese nationals were the masterminds, while the Filipino nationals, including eight women, acted as customer service agents to lure potential victims. The two Malaysian suspects served as the runner and driver," he said in a statement today.
Azry said police seized 21 mobile phones, 15 laptops, a network router, two blazers, several shirts and four epaulettes, with the total value of the items estimated at RM55,000.
He said none of the suspects had previous criminal records and that they had been remanded until July 24 to assist in the investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating, which carries a penalty of up to 10 years' imprisonment, whipping and a fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid urges BN to boost campaign momentum in Negri Sembilan polls
Article:
SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on the coalition to step up its campaign momentum, cautioning that BN enters the state election as the underdog.
"I am still not satisfied with the momentum of our election machinery. We are the underdogs, and so are our candidates," Zahid said after attending the 'Amanat TPM' event with the Negri Sembilan Community Development Department today.
"Our 14 assemblymen have always regarded themselves as underdogs. However, even underdogs have a chance.
"The people's longing for BN must be repaid. God willing, we will repay the people's trust not during the campaign, but after a new government is formed in Negri Sembilan."
Zahid, who is also deputy Prime Minister and Umno president, was responding to questions about BN's campaign momentum on its fourth day.
On allegations that BN was using government assets for campaigning, Zahid said: "Others have used them too. I don't want to name anyone, but I have the records. They shouldn't object when someone they disagree with uses them while allowing others to do the same."
His comments followed reports that Pakatan Harapan's Jeram Padang candidate, G. Manivannan, alleged BN was using public facilities, including community halls, to store party flags and host political gatherings.
Manivannan said his representatives were investigating the claims and would lodge a formal complaint with the Election Commission.
Meanwhile, Zahid also called for balanced media coverage for all candidates contesting in the polls, saying fair reporting was essential for democracy.
He said he had received feedback alleging a "blackout" on certain candidates and political parties.
"I have heard the same feedback. There are claims of a blackout on certain candidates and parties, or that they are being given very limited coverage. I think everyone should be treated fairly," he said.
Zahid added that he sympathised with journalists who covered campaign events only to have their stories left unpublished or unaired.
"I feel sorry for media practitioners. They cover all locations and all candidates, but their reports are not aired or published.
"They are assigned throughout the campaign period, yet the stories they write are not carried. That is disheartening for them.
"So please do not discourage them. Give every candidate equal coverage. That is what democracy is all about," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Afghans search for dozens missing after devastating flood
Article:
PARUN: Rescuers were struggling to reach residents trapped under mud and boulders in eastern Afghanistan on Tuesday, after a major flood smashed through one province killing at least 23 people.
Around 100 people are unaccounted for, the national disaster agency and the provincial governor's office said, also updating the death toll from Monday's deluge.
An AFP journalist saw dozens of people gathered in remote Parun, the capital of Nuristan province, using wooden poles and their bare hands to search for the missing.
Farid Gul Zaheer, a 34-year-old pharmacist, said he had "never witnessed such a dangerous flood."
"It felt like there was an earthquake, and I heard children and people screaming," he told AFP, after getting injured while trying to flee up the mountain.
On Tuesday, a bulldozer was of limited use in clearing some debris in Parun, where the flood had buried multiple shops and brought huge boulders that smashed into buildings.
Parun's mayor and other government employees were among the missing, the governor's office said on Monday.
Zakirullah Zaki, manager of the Spogmai Hotel, said it felt like "an act of God struck us."
"The rest of the hotels, markets, and shops were completely destroyed," the 29-year-old told AFP.
The Spogmai was still standing, surrounded by destruction, and the manager's missing younger brother was found.
Juma Khan Nayel, communications chief for the Afghan Red Crescent Society, described a "devastating situation" with water rising to unprecedented levels.
They have deployed dozens of volunteers who are working by hand, using shovels.
"Right now, urgently, we are supplying some food," water and tents for those whose homes have been destroyed, he told AFP.
Afghanistan's defence ministry said the military had been deployed to the area to support the relief effort, while the United Nations said it was assessing the disaster.
Zaheer, the pharmacist, said he had "lost everything."
"Everyone is traumatised, they didn't sleep all night," he said.
The flooding and rain have also affected neighbouring Pakistan, where 12 people were killed according to Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's provincial disaster agency.
More than 200 people were killed in flooding and rain that hit Afghanistan and Pakistan between March and April.
Afghanistan is particularly vulnerable to climate change and flooding has increased, according to the head of the country's environmental protection agency.
Environmental problems "have severely affected the Afghan people and have contributed to a humanitarian crisis in the country", Mawlavi Mati-ul-Haq Khalis said this month.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah Sultan, Tunku Mahkota visit Media Prima
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  Sultan of Kedah, Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah and the Tunku Mahkota of Kedah, Tunku Shazuddin Ariff Sultan Sallehuddin, tried their hand at reading the news during a visit to Balai Berita, Media Prima Berhad's (MPB) headquarters, today.
The royal duo were guided by news presenters Meor Abdul Malek Raiyani and Azaria Tagaya during the session.
They later visited Studio A, where they were briefed on the television news production process and given an insight into the behind-the-scenes operations involved in producing the country's leading news bulletin.
The visit formed part of the Sultan and the Tunku Mahkota's tour of Balai Berita, which houses Media Prima's various media platforms, including television, print, digital and audio operations.
The Tunku Mahkota arrived first and was received by Media Prima Group Corporate Communications Department and Chairman's Office group general manager Azlan Abdul Aziz and New Straits Times deputy group editor Sharanjit Singh.
[[nid:1494107]]
Sultan Sallehuddin arrived shortly afterwards and was welcomed by Media Prima group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid.
The Sultan and the Tunku Mahkota then rejoined each other before proceeding to Studio Sri Pentas, where Sultan Sallehuddin signed the guest book and a commemorative plaque.
They were then given a corporate briefing by Omnia's chief operating officer Datuk Mohd Efendi Omar, followed by a montage featuring highlights of Sultan Sallehuddin's coronation on July 5.
The Sultan was later presented with a commemorative gift featuring the Berita Harian front page published to mark his coronation by Syed Hussian.
Describing the occasion as a significant milestone for Media Prima, Syed Hussian said the visit was a meaningful moment for the group.
"We at Media Prima have been counting down the days and are deeply honoured and delighted to welcome Your Majesty.
"This royal visit is truly meaningful to all of us at Media Prima.
"It is a historic and cherished occasion that will remain forever etched in our memories and hearts," he said.
Syed Hussian said the visit further strengthened the relationship between Media Prima and the royal institution.
"Today's visit is very meaningful to Media Prima because the visit by His Royal Highness the Sultan of Kedah further strengthens the relationship between Media Prima and the royal institution.
"On behalf of Media Prima, we express our utmost gratitude to His Royal Highness the Sultan of Kedah and the Tunku Mahkota for gracing us with their presence and visiting us at Media Prima today," he said.
The royal entourage later visited the New Straits Times Press (NSTP) archive facility, Klik, where Sultan Sallehuddin viewed the group's digital repository of historical archives dating back to 1845, including bound newspaper volumes, hard copies, microfilms and photographs documenting significant moments in the nation's history.
The visit concluded at Media Prima Audio, where the Sultan and the Tunku Mahkota were introduced to the group's radio recording studios and broadcast operations.
Also present were Media Prima group managing editor Jasbant Singh, Harian Metro group editor Husain Jahit and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Johor govt shuts down Network School, effective July 22
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor government has backed the Iskandar Puteri City Council's (MBIP) decision to revoke the business licence of Network School (NS0 Malaysia Sdn Bhd) and shut down all of its operations in Forest City, effective tomorrow.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the government fully supported MBIP's decision following a meeting to evaluate inspection reports, enforcement findings and written representations submitted by the company.
"Enforcement action can only be taken once complaints are verified, non-compliance is established and due legal process has been completed," he said in a Facebook post.
"The length of time a premise has operated does not mean its activities are in perpetual compliance with licensing terms. Once non-compliance is identified, action must be taken without compromise."
On July 18, Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim said the Johor government and local authorities
would conduct a thorough investigation
into issues surrounding Network School's operations in Forest City.
His Royal Highness said the Johor government and local authorities would examine matters under their respective jurisdictions, while issues involving the entry of foreigners into Malaysia fall under the Home Ministry's purview.
Concerns were previously raised over Network School's operations in Forest City involving Israeli nationals.
Onn Hafiz said while Johor welcomed high-value foreign investments that drove economic growth and created jobs, no investor or entity was above the law.
He said the Johor government and local authorities would not compromise on any legal violations or matters that could jeopardise national security, sovereignty or the public interest.
He also urged the Home Ministry, Immigration Department, police and other security agencies to continue investigating possible offences, including the misuse of second passports, identity misrepresentation and immigration breaches.
Given Johor's strategic location along the maritime border with Singapore, Onn Hafiz said any weakness or abuse in immigration oversight must be addressed swiftly and firmly.
He also called on the Digital Ministry and the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) to review and revoke the company's Malaysia Digital status or related recognition if non-compliance was established.
He added that all regulatory bodies should strengthen their due diligence processes, including background checks, ownership structures, funding sources and operational narratives, before approving foreign ventures.
Onn Hafiz said the Johor government's actions and policy directives had been briefed to, and received royal assent from, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and His Royal Highness Tunku Ismail, Regent of Johor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang aims for 100pc cashless transactions
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, July 21 — The Penang government is committed to achieving 100 per cent cashless transactions in the near future through various initiatives currently being implemented.
State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said one of the key initiatives involves the participation of all 24 state government departments and agencies in a campaign promoting digital payments to expand the adoption of cashless transactions among the public.
“The state government is also focusing on continuous improvements through user satisfaction surveys to obtain feedback and better understand users’ experiences with digital payment facilities.
“At the same time, various publicity and promotional efforts are being intensified through the placement of promotional materials at state government offices, as well as the use of official communication channels such as departmental websites, social media and digital platforms,” he told reporters after a press conference at Komtar here today.
He said the ongoing efforts aim to raise public awareness of the convenience, security and benefits of cashless payments.
Meanwhile, H’ng said Penang emerged as the national champion in the Outstanding Digital Payment Usage Performance Award under the Cashless Boleh 5.0 campaign after recording a cashless transaction rate of 99.40 per cent.
He said the achievement marked Penang’s second consecutive win, following its 95 per cent cashless transaction rate under the previous Cashless Boleh 4.0 campaign.
“With the commitment and cooperation of all parties, I am confident that Penang will continue to maintain its position as the leading state in the digitalisation agenda and serve as a benchmark for other states in Malaysia,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Police bust international scam syndicate targeting US citizens in Kuantan raid
Article:
KUANTAN, July 21 —Police arrested 16 individuals, including eight women, believed to be involved in an online scam syndicate during a raid on a premises near Jalan Tengku Muhammad here yesterday.
Acting Pahang police chief DCP Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the suspects, comprising 12 Philippine nationals, two locals, and two Taiwanese nationals aged between 24 and 51, were nabbed at about 11 pm.
He said that intelligence showed the premises were used as a call centre for online scams by individuals posing as police officers.
“The syndicate is believed to have targeted victims in the United States, tricking them into transferring money through various planned ruses and modus operandi.
“Preliminary investigations found that the two Taiwanese men were the masterminds, while the two local men acted as runners and drivers. The Philippine nationals served as customer service agents communicating directly with the victims,” he told reporters here today.
Azry Akmar added that police confiscated an array of equipment used in the scam, such as 15 laptops and 21 mobile phones.
He added that none of the suspects had prior criminal records and all have been remanded for four days until July 24 to assist in investigations under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating. — Bernama

Title: Sarawak boat tragedy: Three-year-old found dead, three missing after vessel capsizes in Baram
Article:
MIRI, July 21 — A three-year-old child was found dead, while three others remain missing after a boat carrying the victims reportedly capsized in Sungai Seperot near Long San, Baram.
The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said it received information on the incident at 12.30pm before contacting the Long Lama Police Station to verify the report and obtain further details.
“A three-year-old child was found by members of the public. The victim’s body was taken to the Long San Health Clinic,” the department said in a statement.
According to the police report, the four victims, all from Kampung Long Selatong, had left by boat for the Long San Health Clinic yesterday (July 20) but failed to return home and could not be contacted.
The three individuals still missing are two men, in their 30s and 40s, and a woman in her 30s.
Villagers later discovered the boat believed to have been used by the victims overturned in Sungai Seperot.
The department said a search and rescue (SAR) operation involving firefighters and the police will commence tomorrow, with rescue personnel expected to travel about five hours via logging roads to reach the site. — Bernama

Title: Post-mortem report still pending in case of army captain charged over fatal MEX Highway crash involving Bangladeshi passenger
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The prosecution has completed handing over documents for the case involving a military captain accused of driving under the influence of alcohol and causing a fatal crash that killed a Bangladeshi e-hailing passenger.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Batrisyia Mohd Khusri told the Magistrates’ Court today that documents related to two charges under the Road Transport Act had been submitted during the case mention before Magistrate Aliaa Anwar, according to an
Utusan Malaysia
report.
However, defence lawyer Muhammad Nazreen Jaafar Abdullah, who represents the accused, Captain Jad Faid Arhan, 31, said documents related to the alleged murder charge, including the victim’s chemical and medical reports, had yet to be received.
On May 1, Jad Faid was charged with two counts of driving a white Ford Fiesta under the influence of alcohol, causing him to lose control of the vehicle.
The incident allegedly resulted in serious injuries to e-hailing passenger Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, 20, and driver Jailani Sapih, 41, at KM1.9 of the Maju Expressway (MEX) from Salak Selatan towards Jalan Tun Razak here at about 5.30am on April 23.
The charge was framed under Section 44(1A)(b) of the Road Transport Act 1987, which carries a minimum jail term of seven years and up to 10 years, as well as a fine of between RM30,000 and RM50,000 upon conviction.
Jad Faid was also charged with murdering Muzahid Millad, 22, a Bangladeshi national who was Nafisa’s husband, at the same location, date and time.
The charge was brought under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of up to 40 years, and whipping of not less than 12 strokes if the death sentence is not imposed.
The court fixed September 11 for mention of all the cases.
On April 23, a Bangladeshi man was killed after the Perodua Alza e-hailing multi-purpose vehicle he was travelling in was hit by a car driven by the military officer, who was suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol.
Initial investigations found that the Ford Fiesta driven by the officer had entered the opposite lane before crashing into the Perodua Alza, which was carrying two passengers from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA).
The crash killed Muzahid at the scene due to severe head injuries, while his wife and the e-hailing driver suffered serious injuries.

Title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in several states including Kedah, Selangor, Johor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit several areas across the country, including parts of Perlis, Kedah, Perak, Selangor, Johor and Sarawak, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia).
In an advisory issued at 3.45pm today, MetMalaysia said the weather conditions were expected to persist until 6pm in affected areas.
The warning covers Kedah, including Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu, as well as Perak and several districts in Kelantan, Terengganu and Pahang.
In Selangor, affected areas include Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat, while Kuala Lumpur has also been placed under the thunderstorm warning.
Other affected areas include Jelebu in Negeri Sembilan, parts of Johor including Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru, as well as several divisions in Sarawak and Sabah.
MetMalaysia said the warning was issued when there were signs of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour that were either imminent or expected to occur for more than an hour.
The department added that thunderstorm warnings are short-term alerts valid for no longer than six hours for each issuance.

Title: Zahid tells BN machinery to work harder, admits party is ‘underdog’ in N. Sembilan polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has admitted he is not satisfied with the party’s preparations ahead of the 16th Negri Sembilan state election, urging its machinery and candidates to step up their efforts.
He said BN faced the challenge of being considered the “underdog” in the upcoming election and could not afford to be complacent while seeking voters’ support.
“I am still not satisfied with their groundwork. We are the underdog party, our candidates are underdogs. With only 14 state seats held by our assemblymen before the dissolution of the state assembly, we always consider ourselves the underdog,” he said.
“However, even underdogs must seize their opportunities. God willing, that response will come, not during the campaign period but after it, when we form the new government in Negri Sembilan,” he said after delivering a speech at the Deputy Prime Minister’s engagement session with Kemas Negri Sembilan staff in Seremban today.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said BN’s candidates and election machinery must intensify their efforts throughout the campaign period to improve the party’s chances.
He said BN’s manifesto for the state election, which will be launched soon, would focus on realistic commitments that could be achieved for the people of Negri Sembilan.
Among the priorities, he said, would be creating 50,000 job opportunities during the government’s leadership term and boosting the state’s economy by positioning Negri Sembilan as a halal product manufacturing hub.
At today’s event, Ahmad Zahid also presented Excellent Service Awards to recipients from the Kemas headquarters, Federal Territory, Negri Sembilan, Melaka, Kemas Kuala Lumpur Skills Institute (ILKKL) and Kemas Training Centre (PLK) Melaka.

Title: Forest City Network School licence revoked, Johor warns no one is above the law
Article:
JOHOR BARU, July 21 — The Johor government has backed Iskandar Puteri City Council’s (MBIP) decision to revoke the business licence of NS0 Malaysia Sdn Bhd (Network School) at Forest City and order the company to cease all operations effective July 22.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the decision was made following reviews of inspection reports, enforcement findings and representations submitted by the company, in accordance with existing laws and regulations.
“The enforcement action can only be taken after complaints and information have been verified, inspections conducted, non-compliance identified and the legal process completed,” he said in a statement today.
